# exposed.sud0.dev

> A privacy-awareness tool that shows visitors every piece of information their
> browser reveals to any website — with no permission prompts. Everything is
> computed client-side and nothing is stored or transmitted.

## What it does

- Reveals the data a website reads about you the instant a page loads: IP
  address, coarse geolocation, ISP/ASN, device, OS, browser, screen, timezone,
  language, GPU, and canvas/audio/WebGL fingerprints.
- Estimates how unique your browser configuration is (bits of entropy → "1 in N").
- Demonstrates permission-gated leaks (precise GPS, clipboard, motion sensors)
  that a single "Allow" click exposes.
- Explains countermeasures: tracker-blocking browsers, VPN/Tor, disabling WebRTC,
  and denying permissions by default.

## Privacy

- No analytics, no cookies, no tracking.
- Client-side readings never leave the visitor's browser.
- Network/geo details come from the edge server that served the page — the same
  details every website receives — and are not logged or stored.

## Pages

- [Home](https://exposed.sud0.dev/): the full browser exposure report and FAQ.

## Tech

- TanStack Start + React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, deployed on Cloudflare Workers.
- Open source under the sud0-dev organization.

## References

- Privacy Guides — independent, non-profit privacy tooling and guides:
  https://www.privacyguides.org/en/
